Implements the ReferenceError prototype for the ECMAScript engine in the Pebble firmware.

# Purpose
This C source code file is part of the implementation of the ECMAScript engine, specifically handling the built-in `ReferenceError` prototype object. It includes various headers that provide memory allocation, garbage collection, exception handling, and object manipulation functionalities, which are essential for ECMAScript operations. The file conditionally compiles the `ReferenceError` prototype functionalities based on the `CONFIG_DISABLE_ERROR_BUILTINS` preprocessor directive, allowing for the exclusion of error built-ins if necessary. The inclusion of `ecma-builtin-internal-routines-template.inc.h` with specific macros sets up the internal routines for the `ReferenceError` prototype, ensuring that the engine can handle reference errors according to the ECMAScript specification.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `ecma-alloc.h`
- `ecma-builtins.h`
- `ecma-conversion.h`
- `ecma-exceptions.h`
- `ecma-gc.h`
- `ecma-globals.h`
- `ecma-helpers.h`
- `ecma-objects.h`
- `ecma-string-object.h`
- `ecma-try-catch-macro.h`
- `jrt.h`
- `ecma-builtins-internal.h`
- `ecma-builtin-internal-routines-template.inc.h`
